meta.our_scoring_note: Elitez Security scores reflect deck-stated capability (R00, 15 Apr 2023) plus post-deck SSIA 2023 triple recognition (Commercial Special Mention + Industrial Rising Star + Residential Tier 2 / Buyer of the Year via Hillington Green). 24/7 manned command centre at 2 Kallang Avenue (CT Hub) is operational. Adept Academy WSQ MOU live. PWM+ Mark + bizSAFE Star + ISO 45001:2018 + NS Mark Gold + TAFEP credential stack maintained. PSG IMDA pre-approval for tech bundle is the single open item — currently at 4 (active in application), targeting 5 by 2026-Q4. Aviation specialism and robotics services are NOT scored / NOT claimed — neither exists in the Elitez Security stack per the deck.

strategy_canvas.headline_thesis: Elitez Security breaks the cluster on price_defensibility_under_pwm (PWM+ Mark + 'STRICTLY NO double shift' policy + Adept Academy pipeline survives PWM 2024-2028 trajectory while ~120 sub-PWM peers face SACE 2026 / PWM Mark renewal exit) and documented_cost_saving_case_studies (6 named sites with quantified $/mo savings totalling S$14.6k/mo / ~S$350k over 2 years — a sortable artefact no other mid-tier agency publishes), at the deliberate cost of armed_apo_aviation_reach where Certis CISCO + AETOS will continue to win Changi / SMRT / cash-in-transit / armed-government mandates outside Elitez's PLRD scope.

dimensions.label: Compliance credential stack (PWM+ / SSIA / SACE / ISO)

rubrics.price_defensibility_under_pwm: 0 — Sub-PWM-floor pricing; unsustainable under SACE 2026. 1 — At PWM floor with no documentation. 2 — PWM-compliant pricing without PWM Mark accreditation. 3 — PWM Mark + standard 5% YoY escalator. 4 — PWM+ Mark + transparent 6-7% PWM-trajectory escalator + comp comparison sheet for AGM defence. 5 — PWM+ Mark + multi-year price-lock contracts + SACE-ready vendor disclosure pack + named welfare audit (NTUC USE / TAFEP).
rubrics.officer_welfare_no_double_shift: 0 — Documented double-shift abuse; AWOL/no-show >5%/mo. 1 — No published policy; chronic complaints. 2 — Has 'no double shift' on website but no enforcement mechanism. 3 — 'No double shift' policy + above-floor wages + standard hospitalisation. 4 — Explicit 'STRICTLY NO double shift' + NTUC backing + 60-day hospitalisation + named welfare benefits. 5 — All of 4 plus visible TAFEP audit + USE union-affiliated + officer welfare scoring in RFP.
rubrics.compliance_credential_stack: 0 — PLRD licence only. 1 — PLRD + bizSAFE Level 2. 2 — PLRD + bizSAFE Star + ISO 9001. 3 — Above plus PWM Mark + ISO 45001. 4 — Above plus SSIA recognition + NS Mark + TAFEP. 5 — Full stack: SSIA × 3 categories + PWM+ + bizSAFE Star + NS Mark Gold + ISO 45001:2018 + TAFEP + SACE-ready audit pack.
rubrics.tech_augmented_manpower_reduction: 0 — No tech offering. 1 — Resells third-party CCTV without integration. 2 — Owns CCTV + basic analytics. 3 — AI-CCTV + LPR + VMS in-house. 4 — Full bundle (AI-CCTV line-crossing + LPR + VMS + virtual clocking + anti-exhaustion MDVR + facial recognition) integrated into manpower-reduction sales motion. 5 — All of 4 plus documented manpower-reduction case studies with $/mo savings (e.g. Auto Gate + LPR + Remote Command displacing 1 officer at quantified saving).
rubrics.group_hr_training_pipeline_depth: 0 — Single-channel external recruitment only. 1 — One captive channel (e.g. own training school). 2 — Two channels (training school + parent group recruitment). 3 — Three channels including WSQ-funded courses. 4 — Four channels including union backing. 5 — Five-channel pipeline: WSQ training school + parent-group recruitment + blue-collar pipeline (Hey Rocket class) + union backing (USE/NTUC) + NS-recall network — recruitment moat sub-500-officer agencies cannot match.
rubrics.manned_command_centre_capability: 0 — No remote monitoring. 1 — Subcontracted overnight monitoring line. 2 — Own monitoring desk staffed business hours only. 3 — Own monitoring desk staffed 24/7 with 1-2 operators. 4 — Manned 24/7 SOC with virtual-guard-house service offered to clients (replaces 1 night-officer). 5 — Manned 24/7 SOC + virtual guard house + free site risk assessment + free install up to 250m cable + no upfront + full warranty.
rubrics.documented_cost_saving_case_studies: 0 — None. 1 — Vague feature claims, no $/mo numbers. 2 — One quantified case study. 3 — Two-three quantified case studies. 4 — Four-five quantified case studies on the public deck or website. 5 — Six+ named-site case studies with quantified $/mo savings totalling >$10k/mo aggregate, sortable, with named buyers (Junction Nine, Fu Lu Shou, St Joseph's Church, Hillington Green, Hillsta, Ju Eng Home class).
rubrics.armed_apo_aviation_reach: 0 — No PLRD licence. 1 — PLRD unarmed only; explicitly out of scope for armed/aviation. 2 — Unarmed only with subcontract relationship for armed events. 3 — Limited armed event-only via subcontract. 4 — Armed event-grade officers (e.g. F1 / NDP / Airshow event book). 5 — Auxiliary Police licence under PHSO Act + standing aviation / cash-in-transit / Changi / SMRT mandates (Certis CISCO + AETOS class).

segments.name: High-end condo MCST
segments.descriptor: 200-700-unit private condos in District 9-11 + premium suburban (Park Infinia, Hillsta, St Regis Residences class) — committee-led 2-3-yr renewal cycle.

segments.name: Mid-tier EC / mass condo
segments.descriptor: ECs and mass-market condos (Northwave EC, La Casa EC, Sims Urban Oasis, CityLife Tampines class) — price-pressured committees, PWM-trajectory anxiety.

segments.name: Commercial / mixed-use building
segments.descriptor: REIT-managed commercial / mixed-use buildings (Junction Nine, Seng Kee, Fu Lu Shou-area class) — landlord + property-manager dual buyer, single-vendor preference.

segments.name: Industrial MNC plant
segments.descriptor: MNC pharma / semicon / FMCG / data-centre plants (Mead Johnson, GlobalFoundries class) — corporate-procurement audit-pack-driven, demobilise/re-mobilise required.

segments.name: Religious site + eldercare
segments.descriptor: Churches / temples / mosques / nursing homes / hospices (St Joseph's Church Bukit Timah, Ju Eng Home class) — hard budget ceilings, tech-replacement only.

segments.name: Event / ad-hoc / VIP
segments.descriptor: Crowd control, X-ray screening, concierge, escort, ad-hoc visits — short-cycle high-spec engagements (corporate AGM, F1, weddings, gala dinners).

attack_plans.niche_name: PWM-defended MCST renewal wedge w/ Hillington-class refs
attack_plans.icp: MCST committee chair / treasurer at a 200-700-unit private condo or EC (Park Infinia, Hillsta, Hillington Green, Northwave EC, La Casa EC, Sims Urban Oasis, CityLife Tampines class) facing the 2-3-yr renewal vote with PWM 2024-2028 wage trajectory anxiety, AGM cost-defence pressure, and SACE 2026 vendor-disclosure newly written into the property manager's procurement rubric.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Of the ~12,000 SG private MCSTs, ~7,000 procure security via committee vote on a 2-3-yr cycle (the rest are smaller walk-ups, semi-detached, or self-managed). Of those ~7,000, ~3,200 sit in the premium / EC band Elitez has live references for (Dist 9-11 + premium suburban + EC). At blended ARPU ~S$60k/yr per site (Estate Watch Bundle S$5,700/mo × 12 ≈ S$68k less down-tier compression), slice TAM = ~3,200 × S$60k = S$192M. Y3 capture target: 200 sites = ~S$12M (~6% slice penetration) — credible from Elitez's existing premium/EC reference reel + SSIA 2023 Hillington Green Buyer-of-the-Year proof.
attack_plans.why_gap: Top-5 incumbents (Certis, AETOS) are priced out of the typical Dist 9-11 MCST budget point (~S$5.5-7k/mo for 1.5-officer + light tech). Mid-tier peers (Metropolis, Concorde, Soverus) hold PWM Mark but lack the SSIA 2023 Buyer-of-the-Year proof + 24/7 manned command centre + 6-site cost-saving case-study pack that defends the renewal vote. Sub-PWM long-tail (~120 agencies) fail SACE 2026 / PWM Mark renewal — making them disqualified by 2027 in any property-manager-led RFP. Window: 2026-Q3 through 2028-Q1 as PWM-trajectory + SACE-readiness anxiety peaks across the MCST committee renewal calendar.
attack_plans.why_we_win: our_score 5 across 5 of the 6 MCST-relevant heatmap needs (PWM-compliance + manpower-reduction case proof + single-vendor bundle + manned command centre + welfare). Elitez has the named-site reference reel (Park Infinia, Hillsta, Hillington Green SSIA 2023 winner, Northwave EC, La Casa EC, Sims Urban Oasis, St Regis, CityLife Tampines), the 6-site $/mo case-study pack, the PWM+ Mark + SACE-ready disclosure pack, and the 24/7 manned CT Hub command centre. PSG-eligible tech bundle drops effective price to ~S$4.9k/mo — below the PWM-trajectory cost of a sub-PWM 1.5-officer-only contract by 2027.

gtm.channel: Direct outreach to property-management firms (Premier Property Consultancy, Smart Property, JLL, Cushman & Wakefield Property Management) hosting their MCST chair AGM Q&A sessions. SBF + CASE Property Mgmt panel sponsorships timed to Mar-Apr + Sep-Oct AGM peaks. Hillington Green SSIA 2023 case-study walk-through hosted quarterly at the named site for invited committee chairs. NTUC USE union-affiliated co-marketing for the welfare proof.
gtm.pitch: Replace your sub-PWM agency with the SSIA 2023 Buyer-of-the-Year vendor at the same monthly cost — and walk into your AGM with a printable comparison sheet, six $/mo cost-saving case studies, and a 24/7 manned command centre your residents can call.
gtm.pricing: Estate Watch Bundle list S$5,700/mo (S$68k/yr); PSG-net effective S$4,900/mo. Per Agent 3 recommended_tiers — single-vendor admin, one bill, one PSG application. PWM-trajectory 6% YoY escalator written into the 3-yr contract.
gtm.content: Five assets in first 90 days: (1) ungated 'PWM-Trajectory MCST Renewal Defence Pack' showing sub-PWM vs PWM-compliant cost over 2024-2028 with the 6-site cost-saving case studies; (2) Hillington Green SSIA 2023 walk-through video (12 min) with the committee chair on camera; (3) printable AGM comparison sheet template (Elitez vs sub-PWM peer) co-branded with Premier Property Consultancy; (4) PDPA + IMDA-ready 'AI-CCTV in Your Estate' brief for committee tech-literacy; (5) virtual guard house demo recording (CT Hub command centre) for committees evaluating night-officer replacement.

attack_plans.icp: Lay trustee / parish council operations lead at a faith-based site (St Joseph's Church Bukit Timah class — Catholic parish, Buddhist temple, mosque, community hall) OR operations lead at a nursing home / hospice / day-care (Ju Eng Home class — VWO or PE-backed eldercare). Hard budget ceiling, duty-of-care floor, tech-replacement-only path. Cost increase only acceptable if backed by clear net manpower-cost reduction.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: SG religious-site cohort (MCCY-registered places of worship + community halls) ~1,800 (~28% with formal-vendor security per RCEC 2025 = ~500 active sites). SG eldercare cohort (MOH-registered nursing homes + hospices + day-care) ~390 (~52% formal-vendor = ~200 active sites). Combined ~700 active sites in Elitez's scope. At blended ARPU ~S$54k/yr (Estate Watch tier compressed for budget reality + tech-replacement upsell on year 2), slice TAM = ~700 × ~S$46k blended = ~S$32M. Conversion at PSG-net effective price (~S$4.5k/mo all-in for typical 1-officer + tech-replacement-of-1-officer scope) is sharply better than the current sub-PWM 2-officer baseline.
attack_plans.why_gap: Top-5 incumbents (Certis, AETOS) are priced out at 3-5× the budget point. Mid-tier peers (Concorde, Soverus, Metropolis) bid 2-2.5 officers as default and miss the tech-replacement opportunity. Sub-PWM agencies serve the segment today but fail SACE 2026 + cannot tell the manpower-reduction story credibly. Tech-only entrants (Oneberry, Ademco, ST Engineering Urban) lack PLRD guarding licence and cannot bid the integrated bundle. The structural gap: nobody in the SG market combines PLRD-licensed PWM-compliant guarding + AI-CCTV / LPR / virtual patrol + facial recognition + 24/7 manned command centre + named cost-saving case studies in religious + eldercare context. Elitez has St Joseph's Church + Ju Eng Home as live proof.
attack_plans.why_we_win: our_score 5 across 5 of the 6 religious-eldercare heatmap needs (PWM-safe + case proof + bundle + command centre + welfare). St Joseph's Church Bukit Timah (Auto Gate + LPR + Remote Command displacing S$4.2k/mo manpower) and Ju Eng Home (virtual patrol + facial recognition + intercom replacing 1 night officer at S$1.2k/mo savings) are the two named case studies that convert any trustee meeting in 30 minutes. PSG-eligible tech bundle drops effective price below the sub-PWM 2-officer baseline. Elitez Group's parent network (350-staff, USE/NTUC backing) absorbs the small-site trustee handover overhead that no specialist sub-mid-tier peer can match.

gtm.channel: Diocesan Office (Catholic) + MUIS (Muslim) + Buddhist Federation + RCEC (Religious Communities Engagement Council) chaplaincy networks for religious sites. AIC (Agency for Integrated Care) + AMHF (Asian Mental Health Foundation) + nursing-home operator forums (ECDA-equivalent for eldercare). Co-marketing with Adept Academy (WSQ training) on workforce-redesign rebate eligibility. Quarterly site-walk-through invites for chaplains / nursing-home directors at St Joseph's Church + Ju Eng Home.
gtm.pitch: Replace one night officer with virtual patrol + facial recognition + intercom from our 24/7 manned command centre — same coverage, S$1,200/mo savings, audit-ready under MOH or parish-council review.
gtm.pricing: Tech-replacement SKU: ~S$4,500/mo all-in for 1 PWM+-compliant officer + Auto Gate / LPR / VMS / virtual guard house from CT Hub. PSG-net effective ~S$3,800/mo. Multi-year contract with PWM-trajectory escalator.
gtm.content: Three assets: (1) gated 'Religious Site Security Replacement Playbook' co-branded with RCEC covering Auto Gate + LPR + Remote Command economics; (2) MOH-eldercare audit-ready security brief for nursing-home directors with the Ju Eng Home savings model; (3) 'Trustee Toolkit' — 1-page costed before/after scenario template with St Joseph's Church + Ju Eng Home reference numbers ready to drop into a parish council / nursing-home board pack.

attack_plans.niche_name: PWM-compliant MNC plant audit pack at mid-market+
attack_plans.icp: Plant Director or EHS lead at a SG-based MNC pharma / semicon / FMCG / data-centre operator (Mead Johnson, GlobalFoundries class) with corporate-procurement-pre-approved budget against a regional spend ceiling. Cares about complete vendor-readiness audit binder (bizSAFE Star + ISO 45001 + PWM+ + TAFEP + NS Mark Gold) + demobilise/re-mobilise SLA + welfare disclosure for parent-MNC ESG, more than per-officer rate.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: SG MNC plant cohort (pharma + semicon + FMCG + data-centre operators with SG manufacturing / data presence) ~470 sites (EDB SG Manufacturing Yearbook 2024 + DCDA Data Centre Directory 2025 cross-referenced). Of those, ~330 procure security via corporate procurement against a regional ceiling (the rest are smaller plants procuring locally). At Bastion bundle list S$26k/mo per plant × 12 = S$312k/yr ARPU, full-cohort TAM = ~330 × ~S$285k blended = ~S$94M. Y3 capture target: 30 plants = ~S$8.5M (~9% slice penetration), credible only if Elitez wins 2-3 named MNC reference plants in 2026-Q4 through 2027-Q2.
attack_plans.why_gap: Tier-1 incumbents (Certis enterprise, AETOS industrial, Securitas SG, Allied Universal SG ex-G4S, Prosegur SG) carry the full credential stack at premium APO-inflected rate cards (S$4,500-5,500/officer/mo loaded). Mid-tier peers (Concorde, Soverus, Metropolis) lack the full credential stack (especially SSIA recognition + named welfare audit + cross-border tax handling for SEA-mobility) AND lack the demobilise/re-mobilise SLA bench depth. The gap is structural: nobody offers the same Tier-1 credential stack at 15-25% lower spend. Elitez's deck-stated full credential stack (SSIA 2023 × 3 + PWM+ + bizSAFE Star + NS Mark Gold + ISO 45001:2018 + TAFEP) plus 5-channel HR pipeline + WDG(JR+) JR+ workforce-redesign status — at PWM-compliant mid-market+ pricing — is the unmatched combination.
attack_plans.why_we_win: our_score 5 across 4 of 6 MNC-relevant heatmap needs (PWM-safe + case proof + manned SOC + welfare); our_score 4 on bundle + audit-pack (Bastion SKU is purpose-built to lift these to 5 within Y1). Same compliance stack as Tier-1 at 15-25% lower spend. WDG(JR+) workforce-redesign rebate (70% / S$50k cap) is an Elitez-only moat against tier-1 — no other PLRD-licensed agency holds JR+ status. Adept Academy + Elitez Group + USE/NTUC + Hey Rocket + NS-recall 5-channel HR pipeline absorbs the demobilise/re-mobilise SLA on plant volume swings. Mead Johnson + GlobalFoundries-pattern named references already in the deck.

Quarterly EHS director roundtables hosted with a named Big-4 audit partner (KPMG / Deloitte / EY) as guest. Co-marketing with Adept Academy on WSQ-funded 'tech-augmented patrol' training as an EHS-team enablement.
gtm.pitch: The same Tier-1 audit binder as Certis enterprise or AETOS industrial — at 15-25% lower spend, with the only WDG(JR+) workforce-redesign rebate in the SG security market and a 5-channel HR pipeline that absorbs your plant-volume swings inside 4 weeks.
gtm.pricing: Bastion list S$26,000/mo (S$312k/yr); EDG + WDG(JR+)-net effective S$21,500/mo. Portfolio rate-card available for MNC operators with 3+ SG sites. OBC variant: 60% fixed + 40% variable on incident metrics + manpower-reduction-via-tech KPIs.
